feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Master Data Integration - Integrating with SAP ECC and non-SAP Applications
entry.content:
While reviewing the Master Data Integration documentation on the SAP Help Portal -> 'https://help.sap.com/docs/master-data-integration/sap-master-data-integration-prod/integrating-with-sap-ecc-and-non-sap-applications'  , we came across the following reference:

Furthermore, in SAP Discovery Center, there is a mission called "https://help.sap.com/docs/link-disclaimer?site=https%3A%2F%2Fdiscovery-center.cloud.sap%2Fmissiondetail%2F4248%2F4505%2F". It includes the use case to synchronize master data between an ECC System, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Master Data Integration service and SAP SuccessFactors on the example of a cost center object.

However, the link provided for the SAP Discovery Center mission appears to be inaccessible or not functioning.

Could you please verify and provide the correct or updated link for this mission?

entry.title: Guidance on calling S4 OData API from SAP IS using OAUTH.
entry.content:
Hi,

We were looking into options to see how to make the OData calls to S4 API from SAP IS using OAUTH Authentication. Curretly it is working fine with Basic Authentication.

We tried to follow the below blog posts.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/step-by-step-calling-s-4hana-private-cloud-standard-odata-apis-with-oauth-2/ba-p/14346113

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-integration-suite-connecting-to-s-4hana-private-cloud-odata-with-oauth/ba-p/14295261

 

We have couple of questions which we are not very clear.

 

  1.  It seems like a 2 step process to make the OData calls. One Http call to get the token and another ODAta call by passing the bearer token. There is no OAUTH2 Authentication availabe for OData connector when trying to call the SAP Private Cloud API. So, to get the token we need to pass the username/password as the client_id / secrets. This means we need to store the username/password in Secutrity material and pass that for the http call. Will this make OAUTH Authentication more secure than Basic Authentication ?
  2. If we use the OAUTH approach, how is the access to the API works ? The OAuth token will only have access to the API (Scope) configured on the OAUTH client  ? Do we need to grant any roles to the SU01 user to get access to the API explicitly ?  If yes, then the Basic Authentication also works ?

 

It might be easy if we can have a call to clarify these.

 

Cheers

Anoop

entry.title: I want to know how to map a QueryManageSiteLogisticTask to ManageSiteLogisticsTaskIn
entry.content:
Hey, 
I'm trying to create an automation of managing pick tasks in sap bydesign, using cloud integration iflow. I need to use the information from the next web service (Query Site Logistics Task) and insert the data to the web service ManageSiteLogisticsTask while supporting a split operation for different identified stock id values for a certain product and line.

The query response does not contain all the information about the split. I have a table which im saving in the iflow as a property, and this table contains the info about the lines that need to split. 
I have a few questions about rules and parameters of these web services, which I was not sure about from the documentation:

1. Assume I have one Sales Order ID and one task (pick task) which contains all the items for that order.  Does the element "OperationActivity"   include the MaterialInput/output for all of the lines in the order? 
2. I saw in a few payload examples of the Manage Site Logistics Tasks Web Service, that when it's a Pick task, they did not insert MaterialInput values. I thought that it's mandatory to insert something (just copy the same values in both output and input) as the example here. So I need to know if it's necessary or not. 
3. The example in the previous section has the ClearIdentifiedStockIndicator field wrapped in another MaterialOutput element. I want to know if it's the only way, because according to the wsdl the code looks like this :

<MaterialOutput>
<!--Optional:-->
<MaterialOutputUUID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</MaterialOutputUUID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ProductID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</ProductID>
<!--Optional:-->
<SourceLogisticsAreaIDPostSplit>?</SourceLogisticsAreaIDPostSplit>
<!--Optional:-->
<TargetLogisticsAreaID>?</TargetLogisticsAreaID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ActualQuantity unitCode="?">?</ActualQuantity>
<!--Optional:-->
<IdentifiedStockID schemeID="?" schemeVersionID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</IdentifiedStockID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ProductRequirementSpecificationID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</ProductRequirementSpecificationID>
<!--Optional:-->
<InventoryRestrictedIndicator>?</InventoryRestrictedIndicator>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsDeviationReasonCode listAgencyID="?">?</LogisticsDeviationReasonCode>
<!--Optional:-->
<SplitIndicator>?</SplitIndicator>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
<LogisticsPackageOutput>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsUnitUUID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</LogisticsUnitUUID>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsUnitID schemeID="?" schemeVersionID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</LogisticsUnitID>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsUnitQuantity unitCode="?">?</LogisticsUnitQuantity>
</LogisticsPackageOutput>
<!--Optional:-->
<AutomaticIdentification>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
<BarCodeText>?</BarCodeText>
</AutomaticIdentification>
<!--Optional:-->
<SerialNumber>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
<SerialID>?</SerialID>
</SerialNumber>
<!--Optional:-->
<ClearIdentifiedStockIDIndicator>?</ClearIdentifiedStockIDIndicator>

</MaterialOutput>

meaning I should insert the MaterialOutput values and the ClearIdentifiedStockID value under the same scope.
4. I wanted to know if its ok to confirm only part of the lines in a pick task, by sending the payload only with the MaterialOutput of the Sales Order Lines which have been confirmed, and then call the web service again to finish confirming the rest? 
5. If I go by the structure of Manage Site Logistics Task payload I need to understand the hirarchy of the values in the response of QuerySiteLogisticsTask. And then I need to save these values in the table and know exactly which rows relate to which UUID values. I want to know exactly the ratio and predecessor-successor relations between the UUID fields when I have for example one order, one task, two lines, and the first line is split to 2 quantities by the identifiedstockid.

Any suggestions on how to solve this problem or partially help me will be really useful, thanks!

entry.title: CPI keypair as expired soon we are trying to update the keypair
entry.content:
CPI keypair as expired soon we are trying to update the keypair.we have the keypair  provided by the client in the form of XML format but xml format was not been supported in the CPI we need JKS format to upload the keypair

Interface name Single Touch Payroll (STP) reporting - Australia

please refer 2705107 - Recommendation on generation of KeyStore (JKS) file for SCPI

entry.title: SFTP post-processing delete mode in poll enricher does not delete all files from source directory
entry.content:
Hello,

We have an iflow that uses a looping process call to get all files from a directory using SFTP adapter and poll enricher. The adapter is configured to delete files in post-processing, but only 1 file is deleted. How can we do to get all the files deleted? (please note that this is a daily process that can contain more than 5000 files per day).

Thank you,

entry.title: CPI JMS QUEUE CORRELATION ID MANUEL SET
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,


We are using the JMS Send adapter in CPI and would like to set the Correlation ID manually during message processing.

However, the header paramteters below did not work as expected. Could you please help us understand the correct approach for setting the JMS Correlation ID manually in CPI?

SAP_MplCorrelationId

JMSCorrelationID

Best Regards

entry.title: Use of Sequential Mapping Tab in TPM Agreement
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Could anyone please help me understand the purpose of the Sequential Mapping tab in the agreement section of TPM?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: How do I retrieve a CSRF token form SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
I'm trying to program a python script to automatically deploy iFlows to the Integration suite from an Azure DevOps repo. In order to gain authorization to do this, I would need a CSRF token (I already successfully retrieved the oauth token).

Does anyone know how to retrieve the CSRF token? Alternatively, does anyone know if the CSRF token is absolutely necessary and what workaround I might be able to use if it isn't?

entry.title: MS SQL Stored Procedures with Return Codes
entry.content:
This is a SAP BTP CPI iFLow JDBC Adpater related question.

We have a Stored Procedure in MS SQL DB.

In addition to regular output parameters and result-sets, it also return a code (Return Code). This RetunrCode concept is specifically only available in MS SQL DB as far as I know.

The execution/SQL Statement to capture the return code is --> 

? = {StoredProcName} (?, ?, ?, ......);

In the above SQL Statement, the all the '?' (either input or output or in/out parameters) that are in brackets, I know how to handle as it is clearly mentioned in documentation.

However, the first '?' which is supposed to capture the Return Value, I am not sure how to handle that.

I tried the below:

  1. Add an additional output parameter as first one in the Request XML. Did not work, threw error saying number of parameters did not match.
  2. Just sent as many parameters as there are in the brackets with respective fields details. Did not work, threw error saying parameter '0' details/value not set.

I need the return value to be captured in the JDBC Adapter response as the Business logic in further process depends on it. If the value is 1, need to raise an exception and send email to business.

Appreciate if anyone can help how to use JDBC Adapter to invoke the Stored Procedure successfully and capture the Return Code.

entry.title: SAP CPI TPM - Issues with Custom Mappings in new Agreements
entry.content:
Hello all,

Since today I face an issue with SAP CPI TPM. I tried to set up a new agreement on our DEV system which is more or less a copy of what's already running in production mode.

While I have 51 String Parameters for this scenario in CPI PRD I see only 50 in CPI DEV. The missing parameter is the SAP_EDI_REC_Custom_Main_Mapping which holds the information on the CUSTOM_MAPPING I need in this scenario. I made sure to have the latest "Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management V2" and tried to create the agreement from scratch two times after I knew what's missing but this parameter is not being set.
The B2B Scenarios setup dialog did change  and I think that the logic behind this is currently broken. Does any of you face the same issue and/or do you have any idea how to solve or bypass this issue?

On SAP CPI DEV we are currently using these software versions

SAP Integration Suite
Version - 4.8.2
Tenant - ****
Service Plan - standard_edition
    
 
Cloud Integration
6.78.49
  
 
Cloud Integration Runtime
8.42.10
  
 
API Management
2.15.3
  
 
Integration Advisor
2.12.3
  

Kind regards,
Mathias

entry.title: Issue Connecting SAP DMC to MSSQL via CPI (Cloud Foundry) Using Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have integrated SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud (DMC) with our backend SAP S/4HANA system via SAP CPI (Neo). With the new capabilities available in SAP CPI (Cloud Foundry), we are attempting to integrate an on-premise Microsoft SQL Server.

Initially, we tried connecting SAP DMC to the MSSQL server via CPI (CF) by exposing port 1433 publicly, and SAP DMC was able to fetch data from the MSSQL server successfully. However, based on the network team's recommendation, we closed the public access to the MSSQL port.

However, we are now attempting to establish the connection using SAP Cloud Connector, following the relevant SAP blog CPI-MSSQL

In the Cloud Connector (CPI subaccount - Cloud Foundry), we have added the on-premise MSSQL server IP addresses, and the connection status shows as “Reachable” 

In CPI, we have maintained the virtual host and port in the JDBC URL - Ref. screenprint

 

However, we are encountering an error in SAP DMC (please refer to the attached screenshot for the error details).

Error details,

"

{'error" : Root Cause: - Failed to obtain JDBC

Search

Connection; nested exception is
com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The
TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1101 has
failed. Error: "The driver received an unexpected pre-
login response. Verify the connection properties and

LabelPrintDM

check that an instance of SQL Server is running on the
host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. This
driver can be used only with SQL Server 2005 or later.".

Automation Sequences

ClientConnectionld:af429cad-54fc-4b1d-8dc9-
ffe60058025c - The TCP/IP connection to the host

Edg

localhost, port 1101 has failed. Error: "The driver
received an unexpected pre-login response. Verify the
connection properties and check that an instance of

String

SQL Server is running on the host and accepting
TCP/IP connections at the port. This driver can be used
only with SQL Server 2005 or later.".

....

ClientConnectionld:af429cad-54fc-4b1d-8dc9-
ffe60058025c :
org.springframework.jdbc.CannotGetJdbcConnectionE
xception: Failed to obtain JDBC Connection; nested
exception is
com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The
TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1101 has
failed. Error: "The driver received an unexpected pre-
login response. Verify the connection properties and
check that an instance of SQL Server is running on the
host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. This
driver can be used only with SQL Server 2005 or later.".
ClientConnectionld:af429cad-54fc-4b1d-8dc9-
ffe60058025c }"

 

Could you please help us identify the issue and suggest the correct configuration?
 

Advance thanks,

Prabhakar.

.......................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................

The issue was resolved by upgrading the JDBC drivers in CPI to the latest version (mssql-jdbc-12.4.0

entry.title: SAP CPI OAuth2 Client Credentials failing with 400 error — how to fix?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m trying to use OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI), but I’m getting an 400 error when the iFlow tries to fetch the token. I’d appreciate guidance on what might be misconfigured and how to resolve it.

I'm attaching the screenshots of all the configurations.

1. OAuth2 Client Credentials configured in security materials.

I'm calling this in HTTP to fetch token.

I'm also passing Content-Type =  application/x-www-form-urlencoded in header in HTTP adapter.

2. I can see Authorization in Runlogs with Bearer ***

3. Getting 400 error asking for 'grant-_type' in response payload.

P.s : please correct me if I'm wrong, while using OAuth2 Client Credentials, by default "grant_type" is "client_credentials" and it goes in body.

entry.title: CPI logic to ensure that two files are sent together in a single request.
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

In our project for Ariba Remittance, the requirement is to send two interdependent files together as a single bundle. Currently, CPI is sending these files one by one, which is not aligned with Ariba’s expectation. 

we require guidance specifically on the CPI logic to ensure that both files are sent together in a single request. 

Thanks in advance. 

Regards, VS

entry.title: New User for Subaccount - using a distribution list e-mail address
entry.content:
We would like to set up a dedicated technical user in the subaccount for an interface. To do this, we would like to use an email distribution list. Unfortunately, we are not receiving the email from SAP that allows us to reset the password.
When we use a regular mailbox, the password reset notification works.
We have checked the distribution list. We can successfully send emails to this list from external addresses. Only the email from SAP is not arriving.

entry.title: CPI XI adapter removes character + from attachment filename after upgrade past weekend
entry.content:
Anyone else experience issues with missing characters in filename of attachment in messages handled with XI adapter?

After this Cloud Integration upgrade, we noticed that + sign is removed from attachment filename in Proxy interface.

Is this new behavior for XI adapter? And do we have list of illegal characters

entry.title: Read Multiple Attachments in CPI
entry.content:
I have followed this guide line to send email for each attachment. https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/read-multiple-attachments?locale=en-US

The system is sending email for each attachment either with all attachment or Zero attachment.

The purpose is to send each attachment is one email.

Here is first groovy script properties 

AttachmentCount  = 2

AttachmentList = {39AHD.pdf=javax.activation.DataHandler@152e7c48, 0395_20260518135718.102_X.pdf=javax.activation.DataHandler@3359a48c} 

Process loop condition ${property.AttachmentCount} > '0'

I have attached iflow

entry.title: issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2 in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Scenario:

* PO Type: NBIC
* API: API_PURCHASEORDER_2
* Entity: PurOrderItemPricingElement
* Condition Type: PMP0

I am able to successfully PATCH the pricing condition using Postman and receive HTTP 200 OK response, but the value is not actually updated in the database. After re-reading the pricing element, the old value is restored automatically.

PATCH URL:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurOrderItemPricingElement(
PurchaseOrder='4500000450',
PurchaseOrderItem='00010',
PricingDocument='1000000667',
PricingDocumentItem='000010',
PricingProcedureStep='060',
PricingProcedureCounter='001'
)

Payload:

{
"ConditionRateAmount": 159.000000000,
"ConditionCurrency": "AED",
"ConditionIsManuallyChanged": true
}

Response:

* HTTP 200 OK
* No API error

However, after GET:

* ConditionRateAmount still shows old value
* Pricing seems to be redetermined automatically

Observed fields from GET response:

{
"ConditionControl": "C",
"ConditionOrigin": "C",
"ConditionIsDeletable": false,
"ConditionType": "PMP0"
}

Additional findings:

1. POST on PurOrderItemPricingElement is not supported
Error:
"Creating operations are disabled for entity"

2. PATCH on PurchaseOrderItem with NetPriceAmount also returns:
"Operation is not enabled"

3. Same API update works correctly for NB Purchase Orders, but not for NBIC.

My questions:

1. Is pricing condition update intentionally restricted for NBIC Purchase Orders in Public Cloud?
2. Are PMP0/intercompany conditions protected from API persistence?
3. Is there any supported approach to update pricing for NBIC POs through API?
4. Should pricing only be updated indirectly through condition records/info records?

Any guidance or SAP documentation reference would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: How to reuse or modularize JavaScript in SAP CPI?
entry.content:
Introduction

As described in the article Modularising CPI Groovy scripts using POGO , there is a concept of splitting code into smaller modules by using multiple scripts within an ifFow. However, the explanation applies only to Groovy.

I am looking for a similar approach using JavaScript, which is also available in SAP CPI.

POC - additional function outside of 'processData'

As a first step, I prepared POC using a single script. It contains the main function (processData) and an additional helper function defined outside of it. Script below : 

This approach works correctly. After testing via Postman, i received the expected results. 

POC - additional code as a seperate script (failure result)

In the next step, I tried to move the helper logic into separate script to improve readability and maintainability.. So i copied the code into a new script called "JS_ExtScript". Then in my main script "JS_withExtScript", I attempted to use the "importClass" syntax with the path src.main.resources.script.<script_name>.<class_name> .

However, the tests ended with an error . 

 

I did some research and found the following resources: 

- Try use JavaScript scripting instead of Groovy scripting in your CPI project , where someone from SAP recommended to check functions like defineClass, load, loadClass, but none of these worked in my case. 

- SAP Integration Suites javascript load library, similar question regarding my topic was raised, but no clear solution was provided. The author ended up keepin all logic in a single script, similar to my initial POC which works fine but this is not what i am looking for.

 

Has anyone successfully implemented modular JavaScript in SAP CPI (e.g. using multiple scripts or reusable libraries)?
Any suggestions or workarounds would be greatly appreciated.

Looking for you help. Thanks in advance !

entry.title: How to import a Groovy class reference in Resources->References in CPI?
entry.content:
As shown in Modularising CPI Groovy scripts using POGO | SAP Blogs classes that are in other scripts are available by importing them with:

import src.main.resources.script.ClassName

They just sitting in:

 

What happens when I put such a class in a separate package? What is the import path then?

 

I tried to move the class directly to a file in Common Script Collection and the import doesn't work. When the class is in the Local file the import works.

entry.title: Cloud Integration metering for outbound payloads >250KB does not increase Total Messages
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are performing controlled transaction metering tests in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) and we found a behavior that seems inconsistent with the official documentation regarding payloads larger than 250KB.

Scenario

Integration Flow:

HTTPS Sender → XML to JSON Transformation → HTTP Receiver Adapter → External Node.js API

Additionally, the iFlow performs another outbound HTTP call to an external monitoring API.

The flow does NOT contain:

  • Splitter
  • Multicast
  • JMS
  • DataStore
  • Retries

Test Execution

We executed the test using Postman Runner with:

  • 345 iterations

  • Inbound XML payload size ≈ 690 KB

  • Outbound JSON payload size after transformation ≈ 463 KB

We also verified the following exchange properties:

SAP_InboundMessageSizeInKB = 690
content-length ≈ 689558

Additionally, we logged the outbound payload before the HTTP receiver adapter:

OutboundSizeKB ≈ 463

Expected Behavior

According to SAP documentation:

“The combined (aggregated) size of all outbound transactions via one or more receiver(s) adapter is considered for transaction metering.”

“If it is more than 250KB, it will be counted in blocks of 250KB.”

Based on this rule, we expected:

463KB / 250KB ≈ 2 transactions per execution

Therefore:

345 executions ≈ 690 Total Messages

Observed Behavior

The Message Usage Dashboard shows:

  • MPL Count = 345

  • Total Messages = 345

Meaning:

  • each execution is counted as only 1 message

  • payload size >250KB does not appear to increase the Total Messages counter

Additional Observation

When testing integration flows with Splitter patterns, the Message Usage Dashboard DOES increase Total Messages correctly according to the number of split outbound transactions.

This suggests that:

  • Splitter-based outbound transactions are correctly metered

  • but outbound HTTP payload size >250KB in a simple HTTP receiver scenario may not be metered as expected

Question

Can someone clarify:

  1. Is transaction metering in Cloud Integration really based on outbound payload size blocks of 250KB for HTTP receiver adapters?

  2. Does the Message Usage Dashboard aggregate or simplify payload-based metering?

  3. Is this behavior different in BTP Trial vs productive tenants?

  4. Is the 250KB rule applied only under specific integration patterns?

Any clarification from SAP product experts or architects would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks.

Evidence:

Message Usage Dashboard

 

SAP Inbound & Outbound Size KB

 

iFlow Diagram

 

Splitter Comparison
